—Israel gained this victory because God was angry with Judah and made them an instrument of his displeasure. He reminds them of their own sins. It does not become sinners to be cruel. Could they hope for God’s mercy if they neither showed mercy nor justice to their brothers? Let it be remembered that every man is our neighbor, our brother, our fellow man, if not our fellow Christian. And no one who is acquainted with the word of God need fear to maintain that slavery goes against the law of love and the gospel of grace. Who can hold his brother in bondage without breaking the rule of doing to others as he would have them do unto him? But when sinners are left to their own heart's desires, they grow more desperate in wickedness. God commands them to release the prisoners, and they obeyed. The Lord brought Judah low. Those who will not humble themselves under the word of God will justly be humbled by his judgments. It is often found that wicked men themselves have no real affection for those who revolt to them, nor do they care to do them a kindness. This is King Ahaz! That wretched man! Those are wicked and vile indeed who are made worse by their afflictions instead of being made better by them; who, in their distress, trespass even more and have their hearts more fully set on doing evil. But it is no wonder that men’s affections and devotions are misplaced when they mistake the author of their trouble and of their help. The progress of wickedness and misery is often rapid; and it is awful to reflect upon a sinner being driven away in his wickedness into the eternal world.
